title: Bellary Fort
tagline: Bellary Fort — heritage city of Karnataka

overview.short: Bellary Fort ("Bellary Kote") was built on top of a hill called the "Ballari Gudda" or the Fort Hill.
overview.description: Bellary Fort ("Bellary Kote") was built on top of a hill called the "Ballari Gudda" or the Fort Hill. It is situated in the historic city of Bellary, in the Bellary district, in Karnataka state, India. It was built in two parts, namely the Upper Fort and the Lower Fort. The Upper Fort was built by Hanumappa Nayaka, a feudatory of the Vijayanagara Empire.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Bellary Fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Bellary Fort is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Bellary Fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Bellary Airport (3 km) and the nearest railway station is Hubballi Junction (192 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Bellary Fort?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Bellary Fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Bellary Fort?
faq.a: Stays in Bellary Fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Bellary Fort?
faq.a: The top attractions are Kappagallu, Genikihal, Siddammanahalli, Sirivaram, Emmiganur.
